dilemmas.decision_question: Should Hungary sequence measurement/decomposition reform (TP-live-t2) as a precondition before structural gatekeeping/categorical reform (TP-live-t3), or pursue them in parallel or reverse order given ongoing system strain?
dilemmas.evidence_boundary: Further comparative and decomposition evidence can clarify how much of Hungary's identification rise is definitional versus true prevalence change, and can identify which sequencing choices elsewhere preceded disruption (e.g., Poland) versus stability. It cannot resolve how much weight to give present harm to children experiencing delay versus the risk of reform built on unreconciled data, which is a value judgment about acceptable risk under uncertainty.
dilemmas.tension: TP-live-t2 proposes building a reconciled measurement architecture before acting, while TP-live-t3 proposes restructuring gatekeeping and categorical architecture directly. Waiting for clean data risks leaving current bottlenecks, waiting lists, and sorting mechanisms operating unchecked for longer, while acting on structural reform without disentangling diagnostic-practice from true prevalence risks misdiagnosing the problem and repeating Poland's disruptive, non-consensual rapid reform.
dilemmas.title: Measure first versus restructure now
value_poles: urgency of addressing documented harm and system strain
